codex_consult.feedback_summary: Hook blocked codex execution: pretool-tool-policy.py denied write to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ — write target not in allowed_write_path_prefixes for QA role. Same failure mode as iteration 1. Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted: 7 adversarial questions answered — (1) two-change recipe internally consistent, no contradiction; (2) Change 1 replacement wording unambiguous in forced-path context; (3) Change 2 forward-reference in shared resolution section is mode-scoped adequately; (4) Change 1 over-removal risk low due to verbatim target and replacement; (5) AC2 insertion point adequately specified in Technical Hints; (6) R3 Should Have not encoded in any AC, not a blocker; (7) Change 1 is documentation-only, no regression risk. PASS verdict confirmed.

codex_observation_only.note: Change 2 introduces a mode-aware branch inline in the shared Task-id resolution section. This is slightly unusual structurally but adequately scoped by the 'when this resolution is invoked from the Forced-override path' qualifier. Not an objection — structural observation only.
R3 (Should Have) adds a fourth bullet to the 'When to use' section. This is not encoded in any AC and cannot block dev. Observation only.

prior_objections_resolved.prior_title: AC1 does not resolve conflict with close.md line 62 'do NOT branch the resolution logic'
prior_objections_resolved.resolution_evidence: The updated AC1 THEN clause now specifies TWO explicit changes: (1) close.md line 62 — REPLACE the sentence 'The task-id resolution rules are reused identically; do NOT branch the resolution logic.' with text separating form-detection from artifact verification (exact suggested replacement provided in AC1 and Technical Hints); (2) close.md line 139 — ADD a clarifying sentence conditioning the qa-report check on non-force mode. The AC includes an AND clause: 'the resulting commands/close.md no longer contains a textual contradiction between line 62 and line 139.' The Edge Cases section further states: 'The fix must therefore (a) update line 62 to make this distinction explicit... AND (b) add the qa-report skip condition to line 139. Doing only (b) without (a) leaves the document internally contradictory.' The Technical Hints label the section 'TWO changes required (QA objection addressed)' and state 'Both changes are required together. Change 2 alone leaves line 62 contradictory. Change 1 alone does not provide the explicit exception text dev needs to implement the skip.' The contradiction that OBJ-1 identified is eliminated.

prior_objections_resolved.prior_title: BA identified the 'do NOT branch' tension in edge_cases but did not surface it as a spec gap
prior_objections_resolved.resolution_evidence: The Edge Cases section now explicitly maps the tension to both required actions (a) and (b) and states the failure mode of doing only one. The investigation is complete and properly surfaced as a binding AC requirement.

evidence_quality.summary: All BA line-number citations verified against live file content. close.md line 62 reads exactly 'The task-id resolution rules are reused identically; do NOT branch the resolution logic.' — matching BA's cited target for replacement. close.md line 139 reads exactly 'Also resolve docs/dev/qa-report-${ARGUMENTS}.json. Verify both exist.' — matching BA's cited target for the appended sentence. close.md line 94 (forced path permits absent upstream artifacts), line 188 (/do awareness in inspector diff section), and do.md lines 1-7 (hook-only, no post-workflow documentation) all verified.
evidence: close.md line 62 verified: 'The task-id resolution rules are reused identically; do NOT branch the resolution logic.' — exact target for Change 1 replacement
close.md line 139 verified: 'Also resolve docs/dev/qa-report-${ARGUMENTS}.json. Verify both exist.' — exact target for Change 2 append
close.md line 94 verified: forced path must write close-report even if upstream artifacts absent
close.md line 188 verified: '/do' awareness in inspector diff section
do.md lines 1-7 verified: hook-only, no post-development workflow documentation present

scope_alignment.summary: User verbatim requirement contains two clauses: '出现了无法close的现象' (close failure) and '先调查为什么' (investigate why). BA maps these to R1 (forced-path qa-report skip fix) and R2 (documentation in do.md). No universalist claims present in user text. BA did not narrow the bug title, rename the issue, or mark any instance of the user's requirement as Won't Have without justification. The Won't Have list (synthetic artifact generation, /do-specific normal-path logic, pipeline changes) are all legitimately out of scope.
scope_alignment.universalist_claim_check: None found in user verbatim text. No scope alignment objection.

investigation_completeness.summary: BA performed Read verification of both target files in full. Root cause analysis identifies both failure points (line 147 qa-report requirement, line 62 inherited resolution) with verbatim evidence. BA ran two rounds of codex consultation incorporating 10 actionable findings. The 'do NOT branch' tension was identified in edge_cases in iteration 1 and is now explicitly mapped to both AC requirements (a) and (b) with failure mode stated. Investigation is complete.
investigation_completeness.codex_consultation_quality: Round 1: 5 actionable findings, all applied. Round 2: 5 actionable findings, all applied. OBJ-1 and OBJ-2 from QA iteration 1 also applied.

affected_file_accuracy.summary: Both files verified to exist at stated paths with content matching BA's claims. commands/close.md is 386 lines. commands/do.md is 7 lines. No phantom files, no wrong line ranges. The fix targets (line 62 and line 139) contain exactly the text BA identifies as needing change.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.summary: OBJ-1 from iteration 1 is resolved. AC1 now produces an unambiguous runnable recipe: (1) replace the exact sentence at line 62 with provided replacement text that separates form-detection from artifact verification; (2) add the provided conditional sentence after 'Verify both exist.' at line 139; (3) verify the resulting file contains no textual contradiction between the two changes. The 'do NOT branch' instruction is resolved by Change 1 replacing (not appending to) line 62, eliminating the contradiction rather than patching over it.
spec_text_vs_execution_drift.drift_resolved: Prior drift: AC recipe (add sentence near line 147) conflicted with line 62's 'do NOT branch' instruction. Current AC recipe (replace line 62 with disambiguation wording AND add sentence at line 139) eliminates the contradiction at the source.

ac1.notes: GIVEN/WHEN/THEN/AND/NOTE structure is internally consistent. Line 62 replacement and line 139 addition are complementary, not contradictory. The AND clause explicitly binds the two changes to a contradiction-free outcome. Technical Hints reinforce both changes with explicit 'required together' language. The NOTE clause correctly scopes the relaxation to qa-report only, preserving ticket lookup and normal-path preflight.

ac2.notes: GIVEN/WHEN/THEN structure is clear and unchanged from iteration 1. Three deliverables specified: (1) normal-path failure explanation, (2) escape hatch command with path form and /commit next step, (3) caveat about path form vs timestamp form. Consistent with R2 and the do.md file's hook-only nature. Technical Hints specify insertion point as '## Closing /do-developed work section after the existing body line'.

ac3.notes: Only commands/close.md and commands/do.md may be modified. Consistent with development_approach.files_to_modify and excluded scope.

adversarial_questions: Two-change recipe internally consistent — Change 1 establishes principle, Change 2 provides specific carve-out. No contradiction. CLEAN.
Change 1 replacement wording unambiguous — sentence lives inside forced-path step 2, reader is already in forced-path context. CLEAN.
Change 2 forward-reference in shared resolution section — adequately scoped by 'when this resolution is invoked from the Forced-override path' qualifier. OBSERVATION_ONLY.
Change 1 over-removal risk — low, target sentence is exact and verbatim replacement is provided. CLEAN.
AC2 insertion point — Technical Hints specify '## Closing /do-developed work section after existing body line'. Adequately specified. CLEAN.
R3 Should Have not encoded in any AC — cannot block dev or QA. OBSERVATION_ONLY.
Change 1 is documentation-only text — no executable code paths, no regression risk. CLEAN.

summary: BA spec iteration 2 resolves all prior QA objections. OBJ-1 (spec_text_vs_execution_drift: AC1 did not address close.md line 62 contradiction) is resolved — AC1 now specifies a two-change recipe that (a) replaces the 'do NOT branch' sentence at line 62 with unambiguous form-detection-vs-artifact-verification language, and (b) adds the forced-path qa-report skip condition at line 139, with an explicit AND clause requiring the resulting file to be contradiction-free. OBJ-2 (minor, investigation_completeness) is also resolved — the Edge Cases section now maps the line 62 tension to both required fix actions. All five dimensions pass. Codex invocation blocked by tool-policy hook (same as iteration 1); self-review of 7 adversarial questions confirms PASS verdict. The spec is ready for dev.
